<@U0BDN8QJ74Z> New issue — please pick this up.

---

*Issue I3 — Tenant Isolation (multi-tenant scope)*
*Branch:* `feat/s1-tenant-scope`

*Goal:* Establish the tenant-isolation spine: any tenant-owned row is auto-scoped to the authenticated user's `organization_id`, and a user in Org B can never read Org A's data — they see a 404, never a 403.

*Context (read ARCHITECTURE.md):*
- I1 (Organizations + Users migration) and I2 (Sanctum register/login) are merged on `main`. Register creates an Org + a `role=admin` user, so every authenticated request already has `$request->user()->organization_id`.
- This issue introduces: the `BelongsToOrganization` trait + global scope, `SetTenantContext` middleware, and the first tenant-owned model — *Ticket*.
- Ubiquitous-language terms to use verbatim: *Organization* (the tenant), *Ticket*, *status* = open|pending|resolved|closed, *priority* = low|medium|high|urgent, *requester* = user who opened the ticket, *assignee* = the agent handling it (nullable).

*Do:*
1. `git checkout -b feat/s1-tenant-scope` off latest `main` (`git pull` first).
2. Create `app/Models/Concerns/BelongsToOrganization.php` — a trait that:
   - Boots a global Eloquent scope filtering `where('organization_id', <auth user's org id>)`.
   - On the `creating` event, auto-sets `organization_id` from the authenticated user when unset.
   - Source `organization_id` *only* from the session/auth user — never from request body, query, or header.
3. Create `app/Http/Middleware/SetTenantContext.php` — binds the current org for the request lifecycle — and register it in a route group *after* Sanctum's auth middleware so `$request->user()` is resolved first.
4. Create the *Ticket* model + migration (`php artisan make:model Ticket -mf`):
   - Migration columns: `id`, `organization_id` (FK → organizations, indexed), `subject` (string), `description` (text, nullable), `status` (string, default `open`), `priority` (string, default `low`), `requester_id` (FK → users), `assignee_id` (FK → users, nullable), timestamps. Index `organization_id`; index common filters (status, priority, assignee_id). Keep status/priority as *string* columns with app-level validation (not DB enums).
   - Model: `use BelongsToOrganization;`, `$fillable`, `$casts`, and both-way relationships (Ticket belongsTo Organization, belongsTo User as requester, belongsTo User as assignee nullable).
   - Factory: realistic data, wired to org/user via factories.
5. Add the route `GET /api/tickets/{ticket}` (route-model binding) so the global scope is exercised — under Sanctum + `SetTenantContext`.
6. Add Pest feature test `tests/Feature/TenantIsolationTest.php` proving Org A cannot read Org B's ticket.

*Acceptance criteria (must all pass):*
- [ ] `BelongsToOrganization` trait scopes all reads by `organization_id` = auth user's org, and auto-sets it on create when unset.
- [ ] `SetTenantContext` is registered *after* Sanctum auth.
- [ ] Ticket migration runs clean (`php artisan migrate`) with `organization_id` FK + indexes; model uses the trait and has correct `$fillable`/`$casts`/relationships.
- [ ] *Tenant-safety (security-gated):* a user in Org B requesting Org A's ticket id returns *404*, never 403 — existence must not leak. Verified by `TenantIsolationTest`.
- [ ] No controller/middleware reads `organization_id` from request body, query, or header.
- [ ] Full suite green: `php artisan test` (includes prior I1/I2 tests).

*Tests required:*
- `tests/Feature/TenantIsolationTest.php` — seed Org A (user + ticket) and Org B (user), authenticate as Org B's user, `GET /api/tickets/{orgA_ticket_id}` → `assertStatus(404)`. Include a positive control: Org A user reads their own ticket → 200.

*Out of scope (do NOT touch):*
- Comment, Tag, SlaPolicy, ActivityLog models (those consume the trait in later issues).
- SLA logic, policies, ticket mutations beyond the read needed for the test.
- The I5 seeder / cross-tenant headline integration test stays in I5.

*Standing rules:*

<@U0BDN8QJ74Z> New issue — take this next, one PR.

*Issue I3 — Tenant isolation: `BelongsToOrganization` trait + `SetTenantContext` middleware + `Ticket` model*

*Goal:* A user can only ever read or create tickets inside their own organization; reaching another org's data returns *404, never 403*.

*Context* (per the tenant-scope pattern / ARCHITECTURE.md):
- Register flow (I1) already creates a new org + admin user, so `organization_id` is the tenancy key on every model.
- The trait + middleware are built *generically*; `Ticket` is simply the first model to apply them. Later models (Comment, Tag, SlaPolicy, ActivityLog) adopt the trait with one `use` line in their own issues.

*Do:*
1. `git checkout main && git pull origin main`, then `git checkout -b feat/s1-tenant-scope`.
2. Trait `app/Models/Concerns/BelongsToOrganization.php`:
   - Global scope: `where('organization_id', auth()->user()->organization_id)`.
   - `creating` event: auto-set `organization_id` from the authenticated user if unset.
   - *Guard the no-auth case* (seeding/CLI) — no hard crash when `auth()->user()` is null.
3. Middleware `SetTenantContext` (runs *after* Sanctum auth): binds the current org for the request lifecycle. Register it as a middleware alias in `bootstrap/app.php` and apply it to the Sanctum-protected route group.
4. Migration `tickets`: `organization_id` (FK → organizations, indexed) + functional base fields — `subject` (string), `description` (text, nullable), `status` (string, default `open`), `priority` (string, default `normal`), `requester_id` (FK → users, nullable), `assignee_id` (FK → users, nullable), timestamps.
5. `app/Models/Ticket.php`: `use BelongsToOrganization;`, `$fillable`, and `organization`/`requester`/`assignee` relations.
6. A minimal show-by-id ticket route behind Sanctum + `SetTenantContext` (route-model binding) so the scope + 404 are exercisable.
7. Pest `tests/Feature/TenantIsolationTest.php`.

*Acceptance criteria:*
- [ ] Authenticated user in Org A: the scoped ticket list returns *only* Org A tickets.
- [ ] Creating a ticket auto-sets `organization_id` from the auth user — *not* from the request.
- [ ] `organization_id` is *never* read from request body / query / header — session/auth only.
- [ ] User in Org B requests Org A's ticket by id → *HTTP 404* (existence must not leak; route-model binding + scope make it "not found").
- [ ] `php artisan test` — full suite green.

*Tests required (Pest, TDD):*
- `TenantIsolationTest` — two users in two orgs set up *inline (no seeder)*:
  - assert scope returns only own-org tickets,
  - assert `organization_id` auto-set on create,
  - assert cross-org GET → 404.
- All previously-green tests still pass.

*Out of scope:* Comment/Tag/SlaPolicy/ActivityLog models; ticket business logic (status machine, SLA, assignment rules); the full seeded cross-tenant integration suite (that's the headline check in *I5*); role/same-org policies (separate issue).

*Standing rules:* Pull `main` first → implement TDD → run the FULL test suite → open a PR but *do NOT merge* → post a build report (branch, test results, PR link) to `#agent_log` → reply here starting with *<@U0BBYPJ3YP7>* when the PR is up or if you're blocked.

---

:warning: One thing to confirm, <@U0BBYPJ3YP7>: the tenant-scope pattern doc names this middleware *`SetTenantContext`*; you called it `EnsureTenantContext`. I went with `SetTenantContext` for consistency with the rest of the architecture docs so downstream skills reference one name. Say the word if you want `EnsureTenantContext` instead and I'll re-issue the order.

*Issue I4 — Ticket CRUD + Authorization*

*Goal:* Authenticated users can create, list, view, update, and delete tickets within their own organization, with role-based authorization enforced via a TicketPolicy.

*Context:*
- `routes/api.php` — add resource routes under `auth:sanctum`
- `app/Http/Controllers/Api/TicketController.php` — index, store, show, update, destroy
- `app/Models/Ticket.php` — must use `BelongsToOrganization` trait
- `app/Policies/TicketPolicy.php` — role + same-org checks; register in AuthServiceProvider
- `app/Http/Requests/` — StoreTicketRequest, UpdateTicketRequest (validation + authorize)
- `app/Http/Resources/TicketResource.php` — response shape
- Follow `ARCHITECTURE.md` conventions and the `tenant-scope` + `laravel-api-endpoint` recipes.

*Do:*
1. Ensure the `Ticket` model uses the `BelongsToOrganization` trait (global scope + auto-set `organization_id` on create).
2. Create `TicketController` with these actions:
   - `index` — tenant-scoped (automatic via global scope) + support query filters: `?status=`, `?priority=`, `?assignee_id=`. Return a `TicketResource` collection with pagination.
   - `store` — validate via `StoreTicketRequest`, create ticket (org auto-set by trait), return `TicketResource` with 201.
   - `show` — route-model bound `Ticket $ticket`; policy authorizes view; return `TicketResource`.
   - `update` — validate via `UpdateTicketRequest`, authorize via policy, return `TicketResource`.
   - `destroy` — authorize via policy, soft-delete or hard-delete (follow existing convention), return 204.
3. Create `TicketPolicy` with methods: `view`, `create`, `update`, `delete`. Rules:
   - *admin* — full access to all tickets in their org.
   - *agent* — full access to all tickets in their org (manage org tickets).
   - *customer* — can only `view`/`update`/`delete` their OWN tickets (`ticket.user_id === auth()->id()`); `create` allowed (customers can open tickets).
4. Register `TicketPolicy` in `AuthServiceProvider` (or auto-discovered via model-policy mapping).
5. Wire routes in `routes/api.php`:
   ```php
   Route::apiResource('tickets', TicketController::class);
   ```
   Ensure implicit route-model binding respects the global tenant scope.

*Acceptance criteria:*
- [ ] `GET /api/tickets` returns only the auth user's org tickets, filtered by `status`, `priority`, `assignee_id` when provided.
- [ ] `POST /api/tickets` creates a ticket with `organization_id` auto-set to the auth user's org; returns 201 with `TicketResource`.
- [ ] `GET /api/tickets/{ticket}` returns the ticket if same-org; *404* if the ticket belongs to another org (route-model binding + global scope).
- [ ] `PUT/PATCH /api/tickets/{ticket}` updates the ticket; 403 if the user lacks the role/ownership.
- [ ] `DELETE /api/tickets/{ticket}` deletes the ticket; 403 if unauthorized.
- [ ] `admin` and `agent` roles can manage any ticket in their org.
- [ ] `customer` role gets 403 on update/delete of tickets they don't own; can view/update/delete only their own.
- [ ] *Cross-tenant 404:* a user in Org B requesting Org A's ticket by ID → `assertStatus(404)` (never 403).
- [ ] `organization_id` is NEVER read from the request body — always from the session/auth context.

*Tests required (Pest):*
- `tickets/IndexTest.php` — list own-org tickets; filter by status/priority/assignee; empty result for other-org tickets.
- `tickets/StoreTest.php` — create success; validation errors (422); org auto-assigned.
- `tickets/ShowTest.php` — view own-org ticket; cross-org → 404.
- `tickets/UpdateTest.php` — admin/agent update any org ticket; customer updates own only (403 on others); validation.
- `tickets/DeleteTest.php` — admin/agent delete any; customer deletes own only (403 on others); cross-org → 404.
- `tickets/PolicyTest.php` — explicit policy denial matrix: role × action × ownership.

*Out of scope:*
- Comments, tags, SLA, attachments (later issues).
- Bulk operations.
- Real-time events / broadcasting.

*Work Order — I5: Seeder + Acceptance Suite (closes Sprint 1)*

---

*Issue:* I5 — Database Seeder + Pest Acceptance Test Suite
*Branch:* `feat/s1-seeder-acceptance` (off main, AFTER I3 + I4 are merged)
*PR target:* open PR, do NOT merge

---

*Goal*

Ship the seed data and the end-to-end acceptance test suite that proves Sprint 1's headline guarantees: tenant isolation, role enforcement, and full ticket CRUD. This is the capstone — it should be runnable by anyone with `php artisan migrate --seed` then `php artisan test`.

---

*Part 1 — DatabaseSeeder*

`php artisan migrate --seed` must run cleanly and produce:

*2 organizations:* Org A, Org B.

*Each org contains:*
- 1 admin (owns the org)
- 2 agents
- 2 customers
- ~12 tickets total across that org (so ~24 tickets globally)

*Ticket requirements (per org):*
- Varied status: spread across open / in_progress / resolved / closed (whatever enum values I3/I4 established — match them exactly)
- Varied priority: low / medium / high / urgent (again, match existing enum)
- `requester_id` and `assignee_id` MUST be within the same org as the ticket — never cross-org
- Some tickets unassigned (`assignee_id = null`) to test that edge case
- In Org A, ensure at least one customer has 2+ tickets so the "customer sees only their own" test has data to assert against
- Use password `password` for all seeded users; use recognizable emails like `admin@orga.test`, `agent1@orga.test`, `customer1@orga.test`, etc.

*Implementation notes:*
- Use factories where they exist from I3/I4; create what's missing
- Seeder must be idempotent (safe to re-run) — use `firstOrCreate` / `truncate` + reinsert, your call, just don't produce duplicates
- Keep all seed logic in `DatabaseSeeder` or split into clear, called sub-seeders (e.g. `OrganizationSeeder`, `TicketSeeder`) — your discretion, but `DatabaseSeeder::run()` must orchestrate the full set

---

*Part 2 — Pest Acceptance Suite*

Create a dedicated test file (or files) that runs *against seeded data* (use the `RefreshDatabase` trait + call the seeder in `setUp`, or use a dedicated test seeder — match the project's existing test conventions from I1–I4).

*A. Cross-Tenant Isolation (the headline guarantee)*

These are the most important tests in Sprint 1. Every cross-org access attempt MUST return *404, never 403* (a 403 leaks that the resource exists).

- Org A admin GET `/api/tickets/{orgB-ticket}` → *404*
- Org A agent PUT/PATCH `/api/tickets/{orgB-ticket}` → *404*
- Org A customer DELETE `/api/tickets/{orgB-ticket}` → *404*
- Org A user listing tickets → response body contains *zero* Org B tickets (assert the collection is empty of foreign-org IDs)
- Org A user attempting to create a ticket with `requester_id` or `assignee_id` pointing at an Org B user → *rejected (422 or 403 per existing I3/I4 validation — match what's there)*

*B. Role Enforcement*

- *Customer* (Org A customer1): 
  - Listing tickets returns ONLY their own tickets (where they are the requester)
  - GET another Org A customer's ticket → *403 or 404* (match existing policy behavior)
  - Cannot create a ticket on behalf of another user, cannot assign tickets → *403*
- *Agent* (Org A agent1):
  - Can list/GET all tickets within Org A
  - Can be assigned tickets, can update status/priority
  - Cannot manage users / change roles (if that endpoint exists) → *403*
- *Admin* (Org A admin):
  - Full CRUD on any ticket within Org A
  - Can manage users within their org (if endpoint exists from I1/I2)

*C. Full Ticket CRUD Lifecycle (against seeded data)*

End-to-end happy path, one continuous flow:
1. Auth as Org A admin
2. POST create a new ticket → *201*, assert response shape
3. GET list → new ticket appears
4. GET single ticket → *200*, fields match
5. PATCH update (title, description, status, priority) → *200*, changes reflected
6. PATCH assign to an agent → *200*, `assignee_id` set
7. PATCH resolve/close (status transition) → *200*

*I5: Seeder + Acceptance Suite — COMPLETE :white_check_mark:*

*What I Did*

• *Seeders*: 3 new seeders + updated `DatabaseSeeder`:
  • `OrganizationSeeder` — 2 orgs (Org A, Org B)
  • `UserSeeder` — 10 users (5 per org: 1 admin, 2 agents, 2 customers), all with password `password`, recognizable emails
  • `TicketSeeder` — 24 tickets (12 per org), varied status/priority, same-org requester/assignee, some unassigned, Customer1A has 4+ tickets
• *Acceptance Tests* (`Sprint1AcceptanceTest`): 13 tests covering:
  • A. Cross-tenant isolation: 6 tests (all cross-org → 404, listing excludes foreign-org, cross-org IDs rejected with 403)
  • B. Role enforcement: 6 tests (customer own-tickets-only, agent full org view, admin full CRUD)
  • C. Full CRUD lifecycle: end-to-end flow (create → list → show → update → assign → resolve → close)
• *Controller fixes*: Added role-based filtering, cross-org guards, customer restrictions, and `in_progress` to status enum

*Test Results*

• All 31 tests pass, 91 assertions, 0 failures
• `php artisan migrate --seed` runs clean

• Branch: `feat/s1-seeder-acceptance`
• PR #6: <https://github.com/chiruu12/forge2-chiru/pull/6>

<@U0BDN8QJ74Z> — *WORK ORDER: I6 — PulseDesk React SPA (Hero Issue)*

*Branch:* `feat/s2-react-ui` (off latest `main`)

*Goal:* Deliver the complete PulseDesk SPA — login, ticket list/board with filters + search, ticket detail with comment thread, and a dashboard with metrics — fully wired to the Laravel API via TanStack Query. This issue ALSO includes the 3 backend endpoints the UI depends on (they don't exist yet).

---

*Part A — Backend endpoints (Laravel + Pest tests)*

*1. `POST /api/tickets/{id}/comments`* (tenant-scoped)
- Create Comment model + migration: `id, ticket_id, author_id, body (text), is_internal (bool, default false), timestamps`. Add `organization_id` (BelongsToOrganization trait).
- Add `Ticket::comments()` HasMany relationship.
- Controller stores comment: `author_id = auth user, is_internal from body`.
- Eager-load comments on `GET /api/tickets/{id}` response so the detail page has them.
- *Pest test:* create comment (201), verify attached to ticket, verify cross-org → 404.

*2. `GET /api/dashboard/metrics`* (agent/admin only, tenant-scoped)
- Returns JSON scoped to current org:
  `{ open_count, pending_count, resolved_count, closed_count, urgent_open_count, total_tickets, avg_first_response_minutes }`
- *Pest test:* verify counts correct, verify customer role → 403.

*3. `POST /api/logout`* (auth:sanctum)
- Revokes current access token, returns 204.
- *Pest test:* logout invalidates token (subsequent call → 401).

---

*Part B — Design via Stitch MCP (do this FIRST, before React)*

1. Use the Stitch MCP to extract the home-screen / brand design context.
2. Generate each of the 4 screens to match the PulseDesk brand:
   - Login / register
   - Ticket board (kanban + list view)
   - Ticket detail with comment thread
   - Dashboard with metric cards
3. Save the generated design context/screenshots to `frontend/designs/` for reference.
4. Implement the React components to match these designs.
5. *Fallback:* if Stitch MCP is unavailable, use the existing PulseDesk brand (the ◑ logo, slate/blue palette from `App.jsx`) and design clean modern Tailwind screens. Report in the build log which path you took.

---

*Part C — React SPA implementation*

*Stack:* React 19 + Vite + Tailwind v4 + TanStack Query + React Router. Add deps: `@tanstack/react-query`, `react-router-dom`.

*Feature-first structure* (mirror the API):
```
src/
  lib/
    api.js          — fetch wrapper (baseURL from VITE_API_URL, Bearer token from auth store)
    auth.js         — token store (localStorage), useAuth hook
  features/
    auth/           — api.js, useAuth.js, Login.jsx, Register.jsx
    tickets/        — api.js, useTickets.js, TicketBoard.jsx, TicketList.jsx, TicketDetail.jsx
    comments/       — api.js, useComments.js, CommentThread.jsx
    dashboard/      — api.js, useDashboard.js, Dashboard.jsx
  components/       — StatusPill, PriorityPill, Sidebar, ProtectedRoute, Spinner, EmptyState
  routes.jsx
  App.jsx           — QueryClientProvider + RouterProvider + AuthProvider
```

*Screens:*
1. *Login* — email/password → `POST /api/login` → store token → redirect `/dashboard`. Link to register (POST /api/register creates org + admin).
2. *Ticket board* — `GET /api/tickets` with query params: `?status=`, `?priority=`, `?assignee_id=`, `?q=` (search subject/body). Toggle list (table) :left_right_arrow: board (kanban columns by status).
3. *Ticket detail* — `GET /api/tickets/{id}` header (subject, status, priority, assignee, requester, timestamps). Comment thread below: list existing comments, `POST /api/tickets/{id}/comments` to reply. Internal note toggle (`is_internal`).
4. *Dashboard* — `GET /api/dashboard/metrics`. Metric cards: open / pending / resolved / closed counts, urgent-open, total tickets.

*Conventions (non-negotiable):*
- Every API call goes through `features/<name>/api.js` — *never* fetch inline in components.
- Attach Sanctum Bearer token on every request (read from auth store).

- Handle *loading + error + empty* states on every data screen.
- Status pill colors: open=`blue`, pending=`amber`, resolved=`green`, closed=`slate`.
- Priority pill colors: urgent=`red`, high=`orange`, medium=`blue`, low=`slate`.

---

*Standing rules*

1. `git checkout main && git pull origin main`
2. `git checkout -b feat/s2-react-ui`
3. Implement Part A (backend) → run `./vendor/bin/pest` — all green (existing + new tests)
4. Generate Stitch designs (Part B) → implement React SPA (Part C)
5. `cd frontend && npm install && npm run build` — must compile clean
6. `npm run lint` (oxlint) — no errors
7. `git push origin feat/s2-react-ui`
8. Open PR — title `I6: PulseDesk React SPA + comment/dashboard/logout API` — *do NOT merge*
9. Post build report to <#C0BC40Q5LE2> (#agent_log)
10. Reply here in <#C0BC40Q5KED|agent_coder> with <@U0BBYPJ3YP7> when done

*Acceptance criteria*

- [ ] `POST /api/tickets/{id}/comments` works + Pest test green
- [ ] `GET /api/dashboard/metrics` works + Pest test green (customer → 403)
- [ ] `POST /api/logout` works + Pest test green
- [ ] Login authenticates, stores token, redirects
- [ ] Ticket board renders tickets with working filters + search
- [ ] Ticket detail shows ticket + working comment thread (post + display)
- [ ] Dashboard renders metric cards from live API
- [ ] `npm run build` compiles clean
- [ ] `npm run lint` passes
- [ ] All existing Pest tests still pass (no regressions)
- [ ] Stitch designs generated and saved to `frontend/designs/` (or fallback noted in build log)

*Reference*

*Data model:* Organization → User (role: admin|agent|customer) → Ticket (status: open|pending|resolved|closed; priority: low|medium|high|urgent) → Comment (body, is_internal). Every tenant-owned row carries `organization_id`. Tenant is derived only from the authed user — the client never sends an org id.

*Existing API routes (already live):* `GET /api/health`, `POST /api/register`, `POST /api/login`, `GET /api/me`, full Ticket CRUD at `/api/tickets[/{id}]`.
